objectives: Deep performance analysis of mx20022 workspace with file:line references
constraints: Generated code is machine-written via codegen -- changes go to emit/ files
Feature gates exist for pacs/pain/camt/head families
important_facts: 304,904 lines of generated code across 18 files
9,334 format! calls in generated code (path construction + error messages)
6,826 insert_str calls (deferred path pattern in struct validation)
1,470 path.to_string() calls in generated code (violation push only on error)
Codegen already uses deferred path pattern: format! only on violations (snap/insert_str)
Pattern codegen compiles XSD patterns to byte-level checks -- no runtime regex in model crate
ASCII-only pattern detection already optimizes .len() vs .chars().count()
SEPA validator already has is_sepa_char(c: char) -> bool (no per-char String alloc)
IBAN validate_iban line 51 allocates String canonical form per call
IBAN iban_to_numeric line 104 uses n.to_string() per letter
MT parser extract_blocks line 69 converts entire input to Vec<char>
MT parser parse_tag_value_pairs line 346 collects to Vec<char>
Vec<T> blanket Validatable impl line 143 allocates format! per element
Remittance info chunking at mt103_to_pacs008.rs:147-148 double-collects chars
pad_lt_address at helpers.rs:250 allocates String via .filter().collect()
Benchmark suite exists but is minimal (3 parse, 5 validate, 5 translate)
risks_issues: Biggest wins are in codegen (format! deferred but Vec blanket impl still allocates per element)
IBAN String alloc on every validation call even for valid IBANs
MT parser Vec<char> copy of entire input is O(n) memory waste

iteration.summary: Third iteration of deep performance analysis. Verified that several issues from prior analysis have been resolved: SEPA charset now uses is_sepa_char(char), codegen uses deferred path pattern with snap/insert_str to avoid unconditional format! allocation, and pattern codegen compiles to byte-level inline checks. Remaining issues: IBAN String allocation per call, Vec<T> blanket impl format! per element, MT parser Vec<char> copies, remittance double-collect, and missing validate_constraints benchmarks. The codebase is in better shape than the prior report suggested -- the major codegen format! issue is largely fixed via the deferred path approach.
outputs_produced: Performance analysis report with file:line references
next_actions: Fix IBAN canonical form to use stack buffer
Fix Vec<T> blanket Validatable impl to avoid format! per element
Fix MT parser to use byte-level scanning instead of Vec<char>
Consider ConstraintSet HashMap index if constraint count grows

mentions.relevance_note: Created the benchmark suites analyzed here
